revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23989927232|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
'''Amanokumahito-no-Mikoto''' (天熊人命) is a deity in Japanese mythology mentioned in the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Chronicles of Japan). According to the legends, Amanokumahito-no-Mikoto was sent by Amaterasu-Omikami to investigate the aftermath of the incident where Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto had slain Uke Mochi (the goddess of food). Amanokumahito-no-Mikoto discovered that Uke Mochi's body had given rise to grains, livestock, and silkworms, which he then collected and reported to Amaterasu.<ref>https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A9%E7%86%8A%E4%BA%BA%E5%91%BD-2003351</ref> Amanokumahito-no-Mikoto is worshiped in various Shinto shrines across Japan. For example, the Atago Shrine in Ukyo Ward, Kyoto, enshrines him as one of its deities. <ref>https://gejirin.com/src/A/atagojinja.html</ref> Additionally, the Hida Ichinomiya Minashi Shrine in Takayama City, Gifu Prefecture, also venerates him. <ref>https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E9%A3%9B%E9%A8%A8%E4%B8%80%E5%AE%AE%E6%B0%B4%E7%84%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE</ref> In some texts, Amanokumahito-no-Mikoto is considered to have a connection with Susanoo-no-Mikoto. For instance, there is a theory suggesting that Amanokumahito-no-Mikoto might be an alternate identity of Susanoo.<ref>https://genbu.net/data/tango/kumano2_title.htm</ref>
== References == [[ja:天熊人命]]
